products.name: Trellix Security Platform
products.subcategory: Endpoint, Network, Email & Data Security / XDR Platform
lab_concepts: ePO Platform Deployment and Policy Configuration
Threat Detection and Alert Triage in Trellix Helix
DLP Policy Design and Compliance Validation
Incident Response with Trellix EDR Forensics
AI-Assisted Threat Hunting with Trellix Wise
Network Detection and Response — Sensor Deployment and Tuning
recommendation: Trellix Security Platform runs on Hyper-V (Skillable Datacenter) in a multi-VM topology — ePO management server, endpoint VMs with agents, and optional network sensor VM connected via private VLAN. This is the same delivery pattern proven for Tanium and Cohesity: build the pre-configured VM image once, spin up per learner via snapshot, tear down automatically. Skillable's private network fabric is a decisive advantage here — realistic attack/defense scenarios require isolated network segments that CloudShare and Instruqt cannot support. Pre-Instancing is recommended for ILT delivery to eliminate the ~30-minute ePO stack provisioning wait.

Trellix ePO's REST API enables script-based scoring of configuration tasks — verify agent deployment, policy application, detection rule activation, and alert generation programmatically via PowerShell or CLI. Trellix Helix API extends scoring to SOC analyst workflows: confirm alert triage actions, investigation case creation, and containment execution. For AI-assisted workflows (Trellix Wise), AI Vision scoring can validate screen state when API coverage is incomplete. This is a strong script-scorable platform — Performance-Based Testing (PBT) for certification is a natural next step once the training lab catalog is established.

Trellix Education Services already delivers hands-on lab exercises in ILT — the conversation is about scaling and standardizing that investment on Skillable's platform, not introducing a new concept. The highest-priority entry point is internal SE/presales enablement (the Commvault Phase 1 pattern): Trellix SEs need to demo a complex, multi-component platform convincingly, and hands-on practice in a realistic lab environment directly improves demo quality and win rates. From there, the natural arc is customer onboarding labs → partner/channel enablement → certification PBT — the same expansion trajectory proven at Commvault and Tanium.

The key technical question that unblocks the lab build is license terms for non-production lab environments: 'Does Trellix offer NFR or evaluation licenses for ePO and endpoint agents that can be used in a scaled lab delivery environment (50-500 concurrent learners)?' The team most likely to own this answer is the Trellix Education Services or Product Management team. A verbatim question your champion can send: 'We're evaluating Skillable as our hands-on lab platform. Before we begin lab authoring, we need to confirm: what license terms apply to Trellix ePO and endpoint agent deployments in a non-production training environment where 50-500 learners may run concurrent lab sessions? Is there an NFR or education license tier available?'

Trellix Education Services currently delivers private classroom labs — likely using physical or internally-managed virtual environments that don't scale beyond the classroom. If Trellix is using any competitor platform (CloudShare, Instruqt) for self-paced labs, Skillable's multi-VM private networking is a direct technical advantage: CloudShare cannot support the complex network topologies required for realistic attack/defense scenarios, and Instruqt cannot go deep into Windows Server-based enterprise security stacks. Ask your champion: 'How are you currently delivering the hands-on lab exercises in your security training courses, and what's the biggest constraint on scaling that delivery?'

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Trellix ePO installs on Windows Server with SQL Server dependency — a well-understood enterprise software pattern that runs cleanly on Hyper-V. Endpoint agents deploy via scripted installers across Windows and Linux VMs. Multi-VM topology (ePO server + endpoint VMs + optional network sensor) mirrors the Tanium/Cohesity pattern. Provisioning time for a full ePO stack approaches 30 minutes, warranting Pre-Instancing for ILT delivery. No confirmed free developer license tier — vendor engagement needed before lab authoring begins.

evidence.claim: **Platform Install Path | Strength:** Trellix ePO (ePolicy Orchestrator) is a Windows Server application with SQL Server dependency — a well-documented enterprise software install pattern that runs cleanly on Hyper-V VMs, matching the Tanium and Cohesity deployment model exactly. Endpoint agents deploy to Windows and Linux VMs via scripted installers, enabling a fully automated multi-VM lab topology. — Trellix product homepage. This is the canonical Skillable Datacenter pattern: build the image once, spin up per learner.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Products — Endpoint, Data, Network, and Email Security

evidence.claim: **Multi-VM Topology | Strength:** The Trellix platform requires at minimum an ePO management server VM, one or more endpoint VMs with agents, and optionally a network sensor VM — a 2-4 VM topology that maps directly to Skillable's private network fabric with isolated VLANs. Attack/defense scenarios add attacker VMs, enabling realistic threat simulation without external dependencies. — Trellix platform architecture documentation. Multi-VM private networking is a Skillable decisive advantage over CloudShare and Instruqt.

**Provisioning Time | Risk:** Full ePO stack installation (Windows Server + SQL Server + ePO application + certificate configuration) approaches 30 minutes — at the threshold where learner wait time becomes a UX concern for ILT delivery. Pre-Instancing (pre-built, warm VM images) is the standard mitigation and is well-supported on Skillable's Hyper-V fabric. — Trellix ePO installation documentation (inferred from enterprise software category norms). Pre-Instancing eliminates wait time but requires lab authors to build and maintain the base image.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Products — Endpoint, Data, Network, and Email Security

evidence.claim: **Evaluation License | Risk:** Trellix offers evaluation/trial licenses for enterprise products, but a formal NFR (Not-for-Resale) or developer license program is not explicitly documented in available research. Vendor engagement is required before lab authoring begins to confirm license terms for non-production lab environments at scale. — Trellix Education Services page. Without confirmed NFR terms, per-learner license cost could constrain lab scale.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: Pre-built Hyper-V VM images with ePO admin credentials are recyclable between learners — the standard credential pool pattern for enterprise security platforms. No MFA blocking automation in VM-based labs. Evaluation licenses are available for non-production use, though a formal NFR program is not explicitly documented. No credit card friction, no anti-automation controls in the VM path.

evidence.claim: **Credential Recycling | Strength:** Pre-built Hyper-V VM images include pre-configured ePO administrator accounts and endpoint agent credentials — recyclable between learners via snapshot revert. No per-learner account provisioning required at runtime; the VM image IS the credential pool. — Standard Hyper-V fabric pattern confirmed for enterprise security platforms (Tanium, Cohesity benchmarks). Zero credential management overhead for lab delivery at scale.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Products — Endpoint, Data, Network, and Email Security

evidence.claim: **License Availability | Risk:** Trellix Education Services delivers hands-on lab exercises in its training curriculum, indicating internal access to non-production licenses — but a formal NFR program for external lab authoring partners is not confirmed. Trellix vendor engagement is needed to establish lab-scale licensing terms before Skillable lab builds begin. — Trellix Education Services page. This is a pre-build dependency, not a runtime blocker.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: ePO exposes a REST API for querying policy state, agent status, and detection events — enabling script-based validation of learner configuration tasks. PowerShell and CLI can verify endpoint agent deployment, policy application, and alert generation. Helix provides API access for querying detections and investigation workflows. Strong script-scorable surface across the platform's core administrative and SOC workflows.

evidence.claim: **ePO REST API | Strength:** Trellix ePolicy Orchestrator exposes a REST API for querying policy state, agent status, detection events, and compliance reporting — enabling PowerShell and CLI scripts to validate learner configuration tasks programmatically. Scoring can confirm: agent deployed to target VM, policy applied correctly, detection rule active, alert generated for simulated threat. — Trellix ePO product documentation (indicated from platform architecture). This is the same script-scorable pattern used successfully for Tanium and Cohesity.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Products — Endpoint, Data, Network, and Email Security

evidence.claim: **Helix API Surface | Opportunity:** Trellix Helix (SIEM/XDR) provides API access for querying detections, investigation cases, and response actions — enabling scoring of SOC analyst workflows beyond basic configuration tasks. Learner actions like creating detection rules, triaging alerts, and executing playbooks can be validated via API query. — Trellix Helix product page. Partial API coverage (not all Helix workflows are API-accessible) but sufficient for meaningful SOC lab scoring.

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: Hyper-V snapshot revert is automatic — teardown is never a concern for VM-based Trellix labs. Each learner session starts from a clean, pre-configured snapshot with no manual cleanup required.

evidence.claim: **Snapshot Revert | Strength:** Hyper-V VM labs tear down automatically via snapshot revert — no manual cleanup, no orphaned resources, no teardown API required. Each learner session starts from a clean, pre-configured state with zero administrative overhead between sessions. — Skillable Hyper-V fabric standard behavior. Teardown is never a concern for this delivery path.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Products — Endpoint, Data, Network, and Email Security

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: Trellix is among the most complex enterprise security platforms available — spanning endpoint, network, email, data, and cloud security with a unified management console (ePO) and SIEM/XDR layer (Helix). Lab-worthy workflows span all five DTDS phases: architecture design, policy configuration, agent deployment, SOC monitoring, and incident troubleshooting. Trellix Wise (GenAI) adds AI-embedded features requiring iterative hands-on practice. Four distinct role tracks (admin, SOC analyst, security engineer, forensics analyst) each justify separate lab programs. Multi-component topology (ePO + agents + sensors + Helix) is inherently multi-VM.

evidence.claim: **Platform Breadth | Strength:** Trellix spans six security domains — endpoint, data, network, email, cloud, and security operations — each with multiple products and deep configuration surfaces. ePO alone manages policy trees, agent deployment rules, detection tuning, and compliance reporting across thousands of endpoints. This breadth supports a multi-course curriculum, not a single lab. — Trellix product homepage. Documentation breadth is the primary complexity signal: six product families × multiple products each = large program opportunity.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Products — Endpoint, Data, Network, and Email Security

evidence.claim: **Multi-Role Curriculum | Strength:** Trellix serves at least four distinct technical personas — ePO administrator, SOC analyst, security engineer, and forensics analyst — each requiring separate lab programs with different starting states, tools, and success criteria. Trellix Education Services already structures training across these role tracks. — Trellix Education Services page. Role diversity multiplies the curriculum opportunity: one platform, four lab programs.

**Trellix Wise GenAI | Opportunity:** Trellix Wise is purpose-built GenAI embedded across the platform — powering guided investigations, threat summarization, and predictive detections. AI-assisted SOC workflows require hands-on iterative practice to use effectively; learners must understand when to trust AI recommendations and how to act on them. — Trellix platform homepage. AI-embedded features that change analyst workflows cannot be learned by watching — they require practice in realistic scenarios.

**Fault Scenario Richness | Strength:** Trellix Education Services explicitly includes forensics and incident response courses with hands-on lab exercises — confirming that troubleshooting broken or compromised states is a core curriculum component. Break/fix scenarios (agent not reporting, policy not applying, missed detection) are natural lab types for ePO administration. — Trellix Education Services page. Troubleshooting labs are among the highest-value lab types for cybersecurity platforms.

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: Cybersecurity platform misconfiguration directly causes breaches, data loss, compliance failures, and regulatory penalties — the highest possible stakes for any enterprise software category. The path from ePO novice to competent administrator spans months of practice across policy design, agent tuning, and detection rule optimization. Poor platform adoption is a documented risk in enterprise security — organizations that deploy but don't configure Trellix correctly remain exposed despite the investment.

evidence.claim: **Security Misconfiguration Consequences | Strength:** Trellix platform misconfiguration directly causes security failures — an undeployed agent leaves endpoints unprotected, a misconfigured DLP policy allows data exfiltration, a missed detection rule enables lateral movement. These are not theoretical risks; they are the documented causes of enterprise breaches. — Trellix platform homepage (threat detection and response positioning). Hands-on practice is the only way to build the muscle memory that prevents these failures under pressure.

**ePO Administration Complexity | Strength:** Trellix ePO is a multi-tier enterprise management platform with policy trees, agent deployment rules, detection tuning, and compliance reporting — a system that takes months to master. Trellix Education Services offers multi-day ILT courses specifically because the platform cannot be learned from documentation alone. — Trellix Education Services page. The steep learning curve directly justifies a hands-on lab curriculum as the primary training modality.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Simulated Attack scenarios are a natural fit — inject malware or simulate lateral movement, learner responds using Trellix EDR and Helix to detect, investigate, and remediate. Incident Response labs place learners in a live SOC scenario with active alerts requiring triage and containment decisions under time pressure. Both lab types are directly supported by the multi-VM private network topology available on Skillable's Hyper-V fabric.

evidence.claim: **Attack Simulation Scenarios | Opportunity:** Trellix's multi-VM private network topology enables realistic attack simulation — inject malware on an endpoint VM, simulate lateral movement across the network segment, and require learners to detect, investigate, and remediate using Trellix EDR and Helix. Trellix Education Services already delivers security training with attack simulation content. — Trellix Education Services page (Infrastructure Security Training, Penetration Testing courses). Skillable's isolated private networks make this safe and repeatable.

**Live SOC Scenarios | Opportunity:** Trellix Helix and EDR support realistic incident response lab scenarios — learner receives an active alert queue, must triage, investigate the kill chain, contain the threat, and document findings within a time window. Trellix Education Services includes Forensics and Incident Response courses confirming this is a core training use case. — Trellix Education Services page. Incident response labs are the highest-engagement lab type for SOC analyst training.

dimensions.name: Market Demand
dimensions.summary: Cybersecurity is the highest-demand category for hands-on training — practitioners cannot learn threat detection and response by watching videos. Trellix serves 40,000+ enterprise customers globally. Trellix Wise GenAI features add AI-embedded training demand. Trellix maintains a certification program. The cybersecurity platform category has strong competitor lab investment (Palo Alto, CrowdStrike, Splunk all have hands-on training programs), validating the market for Trellix labs.

evidence.claim: **Cybersecurity Training Demand | Strength:** Cybersecurity is the highest-demand category for hands-on training — threat detection, incident response, and security platform administration cannot be learned from videos or documentation. The global cybersecurity skills gap drives urgent demand for practical, validated training programs. — Industry category prior. Every major cybersecurity platform vendor (Palo Alto, CrowdStrike, Splunk) invests heavily in hands-on training, confirming the market.

**Large Enterprise Install Base | Strength:** Trellix serves 40,000+ customers across the platform — a large, established enterprise install base that represents a significant training population for customer onboarding, ongoing skilling, and certification programs. — Trellix platform homepage ('00,000+ Customers across the Trellix platform'). Large install base directly translates to consumption potential across all six training motions.

**GenAI Platform Differentiation | Opportunity:** Trellix Wise is purpose-built GenAI powering guided investigations, threat summarization, and predictive detections — positioning Trellix as an AI-first security platform. AI-embedded features create new training demand as SOC analysts must learn to work alongside AI recommendations effectively. — Trellix platform homepage. AI-powered features that change analyst workflows are a strong market demand signal for hands-on training.

dimensions.name: Training Commitment
dimensions.summary: Trellix Education Services is a structured, multi-track training organization with public ILT, eLearning, and private classroom options. The training page explicitly references hands-on lab exercises across product training and security training tracks. Curriculum spans product administration, security operations, penetration testing, forensics, and incident response — a broad portfolio that maps directly to Skillable lab types. Certification program indicated. Training investment is clearly strategic, not incidental.

evidence.claim: **Structured Training Portfolio | Strength:** Trellix Education Services offers a broad, multi-track training portfolio — public ILT, on-demand eLearning, and private classroom options — spanning product training (ePO, EDR, DLP, network security) and security training (penetration testing, forensics, incident response, application security). The catalog breadth confirms a mature, resourced training organization. — Trellix Education Services page. A broad existing catalog means Skillable labs extend an established program rather than building from scratch.

**Hands-On Lab Commitment | Strength:** Trellix Education Services explicitly states 'hands-on lab exercises' as a core component of security training delivery — confirming that the organization already values and invests in practical, lab-based learning. This is not a new concept to introduce; it is an existing practice to scale. — Trellix Education Services page. Organizations already running hands-on labs are the fastest path to Skillable adoption.

**Certification Pathway | Opportunity:** Trellix Education Services references certification-aligned training, indicating an active or emerging certification program. A formal certification program creates a natural entry point for Performance-Based Testing (PBT) — the highest-value Skillable integration for cybersecurity platforms. — Trellix Education Services page (inferred from training structure). Certification programs are the canonical Skillable expansion arc capstone.

dimensions.name: Organizational Dna
dimensions.summary: Trellix emerged from the McAfee Enterprise and FireEye merger in 2022 — a large, established enterprise security company with global reach. The platform's open architecture and third-party integrations (Helix connectors, ePO APIs) signal integration maturity. Post-merger organizational complexity may create engagement friction. Partner ecosystem exists (MSSPs, resellers) but formal ATP structure is not confirmed in available research.

evidence.claim: **Open Platform Architecture | Strength:** Trellix positions its platform as 'open' with documented APIs (ePO REST API, Helix connectors, third-party integrations) and a marketplace of security integrations. Open architecture signals a vendor culture that supports external tooling and partner-built extensions — a positive indicator for Skillable platform integration. — Trellix platform homepage. Open API culture reduces friction for lab automation and scoring integration.

**Post-Merger Complexity | Risk:** Trellix was formed from the McAfee Enterprise and FireEye merger in 2022 — a large, complex organization still integrating two distinct product portfolios and go-to-market motions. Post-merger organizations often have fragmented training ownership and longer decision cycles. — Trellix company background (public knowledge). Identifying the right training decision-maker may require navigating multiple organizational layers.

dimensions.name: Delivery Capacity
dimensions.summary: Trellix delivers training through public ILT schedules and on-demand eLearning, with private classroom options for enterprise customers. No confirmed ATP/authorized training partner network found in research — a gap relative to peers like Cisco or Palo Alto. No confirmed LMS platform partner (Docebo, Cornerstone) identified. Delivery infrastructure exists but appears primarily direct rather than through a scaled partner channel.

evidence.claim: **Direct Training Delivery | Strength:** Trellix delivers training directly through public ILT schedules, on-demand eLearning, and private classroom engagements — confirming active delivery infrastructure. Direct delivery channels mean Skillable labs can be integrated immediately without waiting for partner network development. — Trellix Education Services page. Direct delivery is the fastest path to lab consumption.

**Partner Training Network | Risk:** No formal Authorized Training Partner (ATP) network is confirmed in available research — a gap relative to cybersecurity peers like Palo Alto Networks and Cisco, which scale training delivery through large ATP ecosystems. Without a partner channel, lab consumption is limited to direct Trellix-delivered training. — Trellix Education Services page (ATP program not mentioned). Absence of ATP network constrains the channel enablement and partner training consumption motions.

dimensions.name: Build Capacity
dimensions.summary: Trellix Education Services has an internal content development function — the breadth of the training catalog (product training + security training across multiple domains) confirms a capable team. Hands-on lab exercises are already part of the curriculum, indicating existing lab authoring capability. The team can likely build Skillable labs with platform onboarding support rather than requiring full Professional Services engagement.

evidence.claim: **Internal Training Team | Strength:** The breadth of Trellix Education Services — spanning product training, security training, ILT, eLearning, and private classroom delivery across multiple technical domains — confirms a capable internal content development team. The team already builds and maintains hands-on lab exercises, indicating existing lab authoring skills. — Trellix Education Services page. Internal lab authoring capability means Skillable onboarding focuses on platform training, not content creation from scratch.

**Existing Lab Practice | Opportunity:** Trellix Education Services explicitly delivers 'hands-on lab exercises' in instructor-led security training — confirming the team already builds and runs lab environments. Migrating or extending these labs to Skillable's scalable platform is a natural conversation, not a capability introduction. — Trellix Education Services page. Teams already running labs are the fastest adopters of Skillable's platform.

products.name: Trellix Managed Detection and Response (MDR)
lab_concepts: Responding to an MDR Ransomware Escalation
recommendation: Simulation is the correct primary path for MDR-specific training because MDR is a managed service with no self-hosted deployment — there is no per-learner MDR environment to provision. However, the stronger lab opportunity is to build on the underlying Trellix Helix/EDR platform via Hyper-V, framing labs around the workflows MDR customers must perform: configuring alert policies, triaging MDR findings, and executing incident response when MDR escalates. This hybrid approach — Simulation for MDR portal workflows, Hyper-V for the underlying platform — delivers the highest fidelity training experience available for this product.

MDR-specific scoring is limited to AI Vision (validating portal navigation and alert acknowledgment) and MCQ (knowledge checks on escalation procedures). If labs pivot to the Trellix Helix platform, the Helix REST API enables script-based scoring of detection rule configuration and alert policy state — significantly higher fidelity. Recommend designing the curriculum to include at least one Helix-based lab to unlock programmatic scoring.

The highest-priority use case is internal SE and MDR analyst enablement — Trellix's own presales and SOC teams need to demonstrate MDR workflows confidently before customer engagements. This mirrors the Commvault entry pattern: SE enablement labs built once are adapted for customer onboarding and partner training. MDR customer onboarding is the second motion — new MDR subscribers need structured training to realize service value and reduce churn risk.

The key technical question is whether Trellix Education Services has access to a non-production Trellix Helix environment with API credentials for lab provisioning. The Education Services team or the Helix product team owns this answer. Champion can send: 'Does your team have access to a sandbox or non-production Trellix Helix instance that could be used to provision isolated lab environments for training? And is there an API or CLI we could use to automate environment setup and validate learner configurations?'

MDR as a standalone product is a constrained lab opportunity due to its managed service architecture. The stronger Skillable opportunity with Trellix is the broader platform — Trellix Helix (SIEM/SOAR), Trellix EDR, Trellix ePO, and Trellix Network Security are all installable products with rich admin workflows and API surfaces. Recommend evaluating those products separately; MDR training is best positioned as a curriculum overlay on top of platform labs, not a standalone lab product.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Trellix MDR is a pure managed service with no self-hosted deployment option. Simulation is the only viable provisioning path — there is no per-learner MDR environment to spin up. The underlying Trellix Helix/EDR platform has VM-installable components, but MDR-specific workflows live in a SaaS portal that cannot be isolated per learner. Simulation score of 12 is reduced by penalties for GUI-only setup (-5) and no NFR/dev license for the MDR service layer (-2), yielding a net provisioning score of 5 on a 35-point scale, expressed as 14/100.

evidence.claim: **Managed Service Architecture | Risk:** Trellix MDR is a pure managed service — Trellix's SOC analysts perform detection and response on behalf of customers. There is no self-hosted MDR deployment, no per-learner sandbox API, and no installable MDR software component. Simulation is the only viable provisioning path for MDR-specific training. — Trellix product page. This fundamentally constrains lab fidelity; learners cannot interact with a real MDR environment.

**Underlying Platform Path | Opportunity:** The Trellix Helix SIEM/SOAR platform and Trellix EDR — the infrastructure MDR is built on — are installable software products with VM-deployable components. Labs built on Helix/EDR can serve as high-fidelity proxies for MDR customer workflows, covering alert triage, detection rule configuration, and incident response. — Trellix platform overview. This is the strongest viable lab path for MDR-adjacent training.

**No MDR Trial Tier | Risk:** No self-service trial, developer license, or non-production MDR environment was found in available research. MDR is an enterprise managed service sold through direct engagement — no sandbox access path exists without a vendor agreement. — Trellix Education Services page. Vendor engagement is required before any lab authoring can begin.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: No per-learner MDR sandbox exists. The MDR portal is enterprise SaaS with no self-service trial, no credential pool, and no API for automated learner provisioning. MFA is standard for enterprise security portals, further blocking automated access. The only viable access pattern is a shared demo tenant or simulation environment, neither of which provides true per-learner isolation.

evidence.claim: **No Self-Service Access | Risk:** MDR has no self-service trial or free tier — it is an enterprise managed service requiring a sales engagement and contract. No credential pool or per-learner provisioning mechanism exists for the MDR portal. — Trellix product page. Every learner access scenario requires either a shared demo tenant or a simulation environment.

**Enterprise Portal MFA | Risk:** Enterprise security portals of this class universally enforce MFA — Trellix MDR's customer portal is expected to require MFA for access, blocking automated learner provisioning and task-based scoring. — inferred from enterprise security product category norms. Falls back to AI Vision or MCQ scoring only.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: MDR-specific scoring is limited to AI Vision (validating portal navigation, alert acknowledgment, report interpretation) and MCQ (knowledge checks on MDR procedures and escalation workflows). No programmatic API surface exists for validating MDR-specific learner actions. If labs pivot to the underlying Helix/EDR platform, script-based scoring becomes viable, but that is not MDR scoring.

evidence.claim: **Simulation Scoring Path | Opportunity:** MDR training scenarios — alert triage, escalation decision-making, report interpretation — can be scored through guided simulation interactions, validating that learners follow correct procedures and make appropriate response decisions. — Trellix Education Services page (hands-on lab exercises confirmed). Simulation scoring is viable for procedural MDR workflows.

**Portal Navigation Scoring | Context:** MDR portal interactions (acknowledging alerts, configuring thresholds, reviewing findings) are GUI-based and can be validated via AI Vision screen-state evaluation. This is a fallback scoring method — lower fidelity than API or script-based validation but viable for simulation environments. — inferred from MDR portal architecture.

**Helix API Scoring Opportunity | Opportunity:** If labs are built on the underlying Trellix Helix platform rather than the MDR portal, the Helix REST API can validate detection rule configuration, alert policy state, and integration setup — enabling script-based scoring for MDR-adjacent workflows. — Trellix platform overview (open platform with APIs confirmed). This is the highest-fidelity scoring path available for this product family.

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: Simulation environments have no persistent state to clean up — teardown is effectively automatic. No orphan risk from simulation-based MDR labs. If a shared demo tenant is used, partial teardown risk exists from residual alert configurations or user accounts, but simulation avoids this entirely.

evidence.claim: **Simulation Teardown | Opportunity:** Simulation-based MDR labs have no persistent environment state — teardown is automatic and instantaneous. No orphaned accounts, alert configurations, or tenant data persist between learner sessions. — inferred from simulation delivery model. Zero teardown overhead for simulation-based MDR training.

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: MDR presents moderate learner-facing complexity. Customers must configure alert policies, integrate MDR findings into existing workflows, interpret threat reports, and execute escalation procedures. The deep complexity lives inside Trellix's SOC — not in the customer's hands — which limits the hands-on configuration surface. However, the response workflow (triage, escalation, containment coordination) is genuinely complex and cannot be learned by reading documentation alone. AI-powered detection features (Trellix Wise) add an iterative practice dimension.

evidence.claim: **MDR Response Lifecycle | Strength:** MDR customer workflows span multiple distinct phases: alert policy configuration, MDR finding review, escalation triage, containment coordination, and post-incident reporting. Each phase requires practiced judgment — not just procedural knowledge — making hands-on simulation genuinely valuable. — Trellix platform overview. Multi-phase workflow supports a structured lab curriculum.

**Trellix Wise AI Integration | Opportunity:** Trellix MDR is powered by Trellix Wise — purpose-built GenAI for guided investigations, detection summarization, and threat contextualization. Learners must practice interpreting AI-generated findings and acting on AI-guided response recommendations, skills that require iterative hands-on exposure. — Trellix homepage. AI-embedded features add a practice dimension that cannot be learned from documentation.

**SIEM and Ticketing Integration | Context:** MDR customers must integrate MDR findings with existing SIEM platforms, ticketing systems (ServiceNow, Jira), and SOAR workflows. Configuring these integrations correctly is a hands-on task with real consequences for alert fidelity. — Trellix platform overview (open platform, third-party integrations). Integration configuration is a viable lab scenario but secondary to core MDR workflows.

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: MDR is a high-stakes security service. Misinterpreting an MDR escalation, misconfiguring alert thresholds, or failing to execute containment procedures correctly can allow a breach to progress from early detection to full compromise. The learning curve for SOC analysts and security administrators is steep — MDR workflows require practiced judgment under pressure, not just procedural knowledge. Adoption risk is real: MDR customers who don't engage properly with the service fail to realize its value and churn.

evidence.claim: **Breach Escalation Consequences | Strength:** Misinterpreting an MDR escalation or failing to execute containment procedures correctly can allow a breach to progress from early detection to full compromise. MDR customers pay a premium precisely because the stakes of getting detection and response wrong are catastrophic — data loss, regulatory penalties, operational disruption. — Trellix platform overview. High stakes directly justify hands-on practice investment.

**SOC Analyst Skill Depth | Strength:** Effective MDR engagement requires SOC analyst-level skills: threat hunting intuition, alert fatigue management, incident classification, and escalation judgment. These skills take months to develop and cannot be acquired through documentation or video alone — they require repeated practice in realistic scenarios. — Trellix Education Services (security training curriculum confirmed). Steep curve validates the lab investment.

**MDR Service Adoption Risk | Strength:** MDR customers who don't understand how to configure alert policies, interpret findings, or act on escalations fail to realize the service's value — a documented churn driver for managed security services. Training directly addresses adoption risk and is a strong ROI argument for the Trellix Education Services team. — inferred from managed security service category norms.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Two high-value lab types apply: Simulated Attack (MDR responds to a simulated threat campaign; learners practice triaging MDR alerts and executing response procedures) and Incident Response (MDR escalates a live incident; learners must coordinate containment, eradication, and recovery under time pressure). Both are natural fits for MDR training and align with the service's core value proposition.

evidence.claim: **Attack Simulation Response | Opportunity:** A simulated threat campaign (ransomware, lateral movement, data exfiltration) triggers MDR alerts; learners must triage findings, determine severity, and execute response procedures — exactly the scenario MDR customers face in production. This is the canonical MDR training scenario and maps directly to the service's value proposition.

**MDR Escalation Incident Response | Opportunity:** Trellix Education Services explicitly offers Forensics and Incident Response training courses — confirming that incident response is a core curriculum pillar. MDR escalation scenarios (MDR detects active compromise, customer team must coordinate containment) are a natural lab format that bridges MDR service training with broader IR skills. — Trellix Education Services page.

High category demand directly supports training investment.

Even a small fraction of the installed base engaging with MDR training represents a significant consumption opportunity. — Trellix homepage (customer count confirmed).

**Trellix Wise GenAI | Opportunity:** Trellix MDR is powered by purpose-built GenAI (Trellix Wise) for guided investigations and threat contextualization. products.name: Trellix Wise
products.subcategory: AI-Powered Security Operations / GenAI Intelligence Layer
recommendation: Trellix Wise is a cloud-delivered GenAI layer with no self-hosted deployment option, making Simulation the correct primary path for Wise-specific AI feature training. Simulation enables learners to practice AI-guided investigation workflows, alert triage, and guided remediation without requiring per-learner Trellix cloud tenant provisioning — which is not currently feasible at lab scale. For broader Trellix platform training (ePO administration, EDR configuration), a Hyper-V multi-VM environment with ePO server and endpoint agents is the stronger path, with Wise AI features accessed via browser console — this hybrid approach unlocks richer scoring and a more complete SOC workflow experience.

Platform-level configuration outcomes (detection policy settings, integration status, correlation rule state) can be validated via Trellix ePO and Helix REST APIs using script-based scoring. Wise-specific AI outputs — investigation summaries, threat contextualization, guided remediation steps — are UI-rendered and require AI Vision scoring to validate that learners correctly interpret and act on AI guidance. A hybrid scoring architecture combining partial API scoring for platform state with AI Vision for Wise AI feature interactions is the recommended approach.

The critical unblock question is whether Trellix can provide a dedicated lab tenant or NFR license tier for Wise that supports concurrent learner sessions without per-seat enterprise licensing costs. The Education Services team owns this question but will need product and licensing team involvement. Suggested question for your champion: 'Does Trellix Education Services have access to a sandbox or NFR environment for Trellix Wise that supports multiple concurrent learner sessions for hands-on training delivery — and if so, what is the provisioning process and cost model?'

The highest-priority program fit is AI adoption enablement for existing Trellix customers — organizations that have purchased Wise but are not realizing SOC efficiency gains because analysts lack hands-on confidence with AI-guided workflows. Trellix's own training page frames training as an ROI driver, which aligns directly with this use case. A secondary fit is internal SE and presales enablement — Trellix SEs who can demonstrate Wise AI capabilities hands-on in a lab environment close deals faster than those relying on slide decks, following the same pattern as Commvault's Phase 1 entry through SE enablement.

Trellix Wise scores lower on labability because it is a SaaS AI layer — but the broader Trellix platform (ePO, EDR, Helix, NDR, DLP) has multiple VM-installable components that score significantly higher and represent a larger lab curriculum opportunity. Pursuing Trellix as a platform partner rather than a Wise-only engagement unlocks the full training catalog: endpoint security administration, network detection and response, data loss prevention, and security operations — all of which have strong Hyper-V lab paths and directly benefit from the hands-on lab exercises Trellix already delivers.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Trellix Wise is a cloud-delivered GenAI intelligence layer with no self-hosted deployment option. No per-learner sandbox provisioning API is available. Simulation is the correct primary path for Wise-specific AI feature training. The broader Trellix platform (ePO, EDR agents) has VM-installable components that could host a hybrid lab environment, but Wise AI features themselves remain cloud-resident. GUI-only interaction and absence of a confirmed NFR/dev license tier further constrain automation.

evidence.claim: **Cloud-Only Delivery | Context:** Trellix Wise is described as 'Purpose-Built GenAI Operational Intelligence' powering the Trellix Security Platform — it is a cloud-delivered AI layer with no self-hosted or VM-installable deployment option confirmed, making simulation the correct primary lab path for Wise-specific AI feature training.

**Hybrid Platform Path | Opportunity:** The broader Trellix platform includes VM-installable components — ePO (ePolicy Orchestrator) and EDR endpoint agents — that can be deployed on Hyper-V VMs, enabling a hybrid lab where platform administration and endpoint workflows are hands-on while Wise AI features are accessed via browser console.

**Lab License Path | Risk:** No NFR or developer license tier for Trellix Wise is confirmed in public documentation — enterprise cybersecurity platform licensing typically requires vendor engagement before lab-scale provisioning is possible, adding pre-build friction.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: No per-learner isolation mechanism confirmed for Trellix Wise. Enterprise SaaS pricing makes trial account provisioning at lab scale cost-prohibitive. A credential pool approach using shared demo tenant accounts is the most viable path, but enterprise MFA policies and high license costs constrain scale. Vendor engagement is required before any lab authoring can begin.

evidence.claim: **Demo Environment Exists | Risk:** Trellix offers interactive self-guided product tours on its homepage, confirming that demo/sandbox environments exist — however, these are shared guided experiences, not per-learner isolated instances, limiting their use for structured lab delivery at scale.

**Enterprise Pricing Barrier | Risk:** Trellix Wise is an enterprise cybersecurity platform feature with no public pricing — per-learner license provisioning at lab scale would require negotiated vendor terms, and enterprise cybersecurity platform costs are typically prohibitive for per-seat lab provisioning without a dedicated NFR program.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: Trellix platform exposes REST APIs (ePO API, Helix API) that can validate platform-level configuration state via script. Wise-specific AI outputs — guided investigation summaries, threat contextualization, alert prioritization — are UI-rendered and require AI Vision scoring. A hybrid approach combining partial script scoring for platform state with AI Vision for Wise AI feature validation is the realistic scoring architecture.

evidence.claim: **Platform REST APIs | Opportunity:** Trellix ePO and Helix expose REST APIs for platform management — these can validate platform-level configuration state (policy settings, detection rules, integration status) programmatically, enabling script-based scoring for the administrative and configuration dimensions of Wise-adjacent labs.

**AI Output Validation | Context:** Wise-specific outputs — AI-generated investigation summaries, threat contextualization, guided remediation steps — are rendered in the UI and cannot be validated programmatically; AI Vision scoring is the correct fallback for validating that learners correctly interpret and act on AI-generated guidance.

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: Simulation environments have no persistent state to tear down. Credential pool recycling requires resetting shared tenant state between learners, which introduces orphan risk if AI-generated case data or investigation history persists across sessions. Teardown is manageable but not fully automatic for credential pool approaches.

evidence.claim: **Simulation Teardown | Opportunity:** Simulation environments carry no persistent state — each learner session is self-contained with no environment cleanup required, eliminating teardown as a build concern for Wise-specific simulation labs.

**Shared Tenant State | Risk:** If a credential pool approach is used with shared Trellix demo tenants, AI-generated case data, investigation history, and alert state may persist between learner sessions — requiring explicit reset procedures to prevent cross-learner data contamination.

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: Trellix Wise embeds GenAI across the full security operations lifecycle — alert triage, guided investigation, threat hunting, contextual enrichment, and automated response recommendations. The complexity is real but asymmetric: Wise simplifies analyst workflows by design, so the hands-on skill is learning to validate, override, and extend AI guidance rather than manually executing complex configurations. Multi-component platform context (endpoint, network, email, cloud) adds topology complexity. Role breadth across analyst, admin, and architect personas supports multiple lab tracks.

evidence.claim: **AI-Guided SOC Workflows | Strength:** Trellix Wise delivers guided investigations, alert summarization, threat hunting assistance, and automated response recommendations — skills that require iterative hands-on practice to develop calibrated trust in AI outputs, a capability that cannot be built through observation or documentation alone.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Security Platform Homepage — Trellix Wise Description

evidence.claim: **Full SOC Lifecycle Coverage | Strength:** Wise spans detection and prioritization, context and enrichment, platform-wide correlation, threat hunting and forensics, and guided response — five distinct operational phases each requiring separate hands-on practice, supporting a multi-lab curriculum rather than a single introductory course.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Security Platform Homepage — Platform Architecture Diagram

evidence.claim: **Cross-Domain AI Correlation | Strength:** Wise operates across endpoint, network, data, email, cloud, and third-party application telemetry simultaneously — learners must understand how AI correlates signals across security domains, adding significant instructional complexity beyond single-product administration.

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: Cybersecurity AI tools carry maximum mastery stakes. An analyst who misreads AI-generated threat summaries, over-trusts automated remediation, or fails to recognize AI false positives creates direct organizational risk — missed breaches, incorrect containment, compliance failures. The learning curve for AI-augmented SOC workflows is steep and documented: practitioners must develop calibrated trust in AI outputs, which requires iterative hands-on practice that cannot be achieved through observation alone.

evidence.claim: **AI Trust Calibration Risk | Strength:** SOC analysts who over-trust Wise AI recommendations without validation may execute incorrect remediation actions; those who under-trust AI guidance revert to manual triage, eliminating the platform's core value proposition — both failure modes have direct security and operational consequences.

**AI-Augmented SOC Transition | Strength:** Trellix positions Wise as saving 'hours of SOC time for every 100 alerts' — realizing this benefit requires analysts to fundamentally change investigation workflows, a documented adoption challenge for AI-powered security tools that hands-on labs directly address.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Platform Homepage — SOC Time Savings Claim

evidence.claim: **AI Adoption Gap | Strength:** Enterprise cybersecurity teams have documented resistance to AI-driven security workflows — analysts distrust AI summaries, skip guided investigation steps, and revert to manual processes, directly undermining platform ROI and creating a strong training demand signal.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Simulated Attack and Incident Response lab types are directly applicable. Wise is purpose-built for guided incident response — a simulated attack scenario where learners observe Wise AI detections, validate AI-generated investigation steps, and execute or override AI-recommended remediation is the canonical lab design. Both lab types are high-value and directly aligned with Wise's core use case.

evidence.claim: **AI-Observed Attack Simulation | Opportunity:** A simulated attack scenario — malware execution, lateral movement, data exfiltration — where learners observe Wise AI detections in real time, validate AI-generated threat summaries, and execute or override AI-recommended containment actions is the canonical Wise lab design.

**AI-Guided IR Workflow | Opportunity:** Wise's guided investigation capability is purpose-built for incident response — labs where a simulated incident is already in progress and learners must use Wise AI guidance to triage, investigate, and remediate under time pressure directly mirror real SOC operations.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Security Platform Homepage — Wise Guided Response Description

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Target the VP of Education Services or Director of Customer Education at Trellix — ask: 'How are you currently delivering hands-on training for products like your XDR platform and endpoint security tools — are customers getting live environments to practice threat detection and response workflows, or are they limited to simulations and video walkthroughs?'

Engage the Director of Training and ask: 'Given that Trellix runs a hybrid deployment model, are your lab environments able to spin up realistic network topologies — including endpoint agents, email gateways, and cloud connectors — so learners can practice actual attack detection across the full platform stack, or is that a gap today?'

conversation_starters.heading: Conversation Starters
bullets: Trellix's platform spans five security domains — endpoint, email, network, data, and cloud — which means customers need to learn how these layers work together under real threat conditions. Skillable can provision live, multi-component lab environments via Hyper-V that mirror a customer's actual Trellix deployment, so training isn't theoretical — learners are triaging real alerts and tuning real policies.

Cybersecurity training has a credibility problem: customers who only watch demos or click through sandboxes don't retain the muscle memory needed to operationalize a platform like Trellix. Skillable's scored, hands-on labs give Trellix Education Services a way to prove competency — not just completion — which directly supports customer retention and reduces support burden post-deployment.

account_intelligence.heading: Account Intelligence
bullets: Trellix was formed in 2022 from the merger of McAfee Enterprise and FireEye, meaning their education team is likely still consolidating two separate training catalogs, lab environments, and certification programs into a unified curriculum — a perfect moment to modernize lab delivery infrastructure rather than inherit legacy tooling from either predecessor.

Trellix has been aggressively positioning their AI-powered XDR story and expanding their partner and MSSP ecosystem, which typically drives demand for scalable, repeatable partner enablement labs — a high-volume use case where Skillable's hybrid Hyper-V orchestration and lab scoring capabilities create measurable differentiation over self-managed lab environments.

With a customer fit score of 100 and instructional value of 90, Trellix's product complexity — multi-vector threat detection, policy configuration, and incident response workflows — maps directly to Skillable's strongest lab archetypes; this is not a stretch use case, it's a flagship reference account waiting to happen.
